japanese: アイスストロベリー焙じ茶ラテ

region_of_origin: Japan (hojicha origin); served as a Starbucks Singapore menu item
food_subgroup: Iced milk tea and latte beverages

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for iced hojicha latte with strawberry and cold foam. FOODON_03301099 (tea-based beverage) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP (Tea-based beverages) is the closest parent. No specific code exists for iced hojicha latte with strawberry flavouring and cold foam topping.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226529007 (Tea drink) is the closest defensible parent. No specific concept exists for hojicha latte.

preparation_method: hojicha powder mixed with low-fat milk

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard grande-sized serving (approximately 490 g / 473 ml) as served at Starbucks Singapore, comprising iced hojicha-flavoured milk base with strawberry sauce and hojicha cold foam topping.

nutrient_source.title: Starbucks Iced Hojicha Latte with Low-Fat Milk — nutritional reference estimated from USDA FoodData Central component foods: low-fat milk (NDB 01082), strawberry syrup (NDB 19351), hojicha roasted green tea (analogous to brewed green tea NDB 14355), and cold foam (analogous to low-fat milk foam)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (Foundation Foods / SR Legacy) — component-based estimation
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA Branded Food entry exists for this specific Starbucks Singapore menu item. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component analysis: approximately 60% low-fat milk, 15% strawberry sauce/syrup, 20% hojicha cold foam (low-fat milk base), 5% brewed hojicha tea, served over ice. Per-100g values are derived from this blend. Energy cross-checked against publicly available Starbucks Singapore nutritional disclosure (grande size ~200 kcal total, yielding ~41 kcal/100g). Values represent a medium-confidence estimate.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of milk-based beverages and sweetened dairy drinks — analogous estimate based on low-fat milk (GI ~32) and strawberry-flavoured sweetened milk beverage (GI ~37); University of Sydney GI Database reference entries for dairy-based beverages
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Iced Strawberry Hojicha Latte. GI estimated at 35 based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for low-fat milk (GI 32, tested) and flavoured sweetened milk beverages (GI 34–40 range). Hojicha tea component has negligible glycemic contribution. Strawberry sauce adds simple sugars, raising GI slightly above plain milk. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (35 × 30.4) / 100 ≈ 10.6, rounded to 10.7.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content (~38 mg/100g; ~186 mg per grande serving)
hypertension.guidance: The sodium content of approximately 186 mg per grande serving is within acceptable limits for individuals with hypertension, but those on strict sodium-restricted diets (<1500 mg/day) should account for this beverage as part of their daily sodium budget and be mindful of added syrups that may vary by preparation.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added sugar from strawberry sauce (~5.8 g sugar/100g; ~28 g per grande serving) and moderate glycemic load (~10.7 per serving)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this beverage with caution due to approximately 28 g of sugar per grande serving, predominantly from strawberry sauce; requesting reduced syrup or sugar-free alternatives and monitoring postprandial glucose response is strongly advised.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (~0.6 g/100g; ~2.9 g per serving) and presence of hojicha polyphenols with potential antioxidant benefit
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The low saturated fat content and the antioxidant polyphenols present in hojicha (roasted green tea) make this beverage a relatively heart-friendly choice compared to full-fat dairy lattes, though the added sugar from strawberry sauce should be moderated in a cardiovascular-protective dietary pattern.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Iced Strawberry Hojicha Latte is a chilled Japanese-inspired tea latte beverage served at Starbucks Singapore. It is composed of a hojicha (roasted green tea) flavoured base blended with low-fat milk and layered over ice, with a sweet strawberry sauce incorporated for fruity flavour, and finished with a smooth hojicha-flavoured cold foam topping. Hojicha is a Japanese roasted green tea made from bancha or kukicha leaves roasted at high temperature, producing a warm, toasty, slightly caramel-like flavour profile that is lower in caffeine than standard green tea. The strawberry sauce introduces a bright, sweet-tart contrast to the earthy hojicha notes, while the cold foam adds a creamy, airy texture on top. The beverage is served cold and is characterised by a visually appealing layered appearance with pink strawberry tones beneath the pale brown hojicha foam. Nutritionally, a grande serving (approximately 490 g) provides around 200 kcal, 8.8 g protein, 4.4 g fat, and approximately 30 g of carbohydrates, with the majority of sugars derived from the strawberry sauce. The glycemic index is estimated at 35 (low GI), though the glycemic load per serving is moderate at approximately 10.7 due to the serving volume. The beverage contains milk and is therefore not suitable for those with dairy allergies or lactose intolerance. It is vegetarian and halal-suitable. Clinically, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should moderate intake due to added sugars, while the low saturated fat content makes it relatively suitable for cardiovascular health when consumed occasionally.

confidence_reason: No direct entry for this specific Starbucks Singapore menu item exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods, or ICMR-NIN. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component analysis of constituent ingredients (low-fat milk, strawberry syrup, hojicha tea, cold foam) using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data, cross-referenced against publicly available Starbucks Singapore nutritional disclosures. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database dairy beverage entries. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are component-estimated; no exact branded entry was found in approved primary sources for this specific Starbucks Singapore product. Values should be updated if Starbucks Singapore publishes official nutritional data in an approved source format. 2. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero by product formulation; low-fat milk and hojicha contain negligible industrially produced trans fats. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g (0.2) and polyunsaturated_fat_g (0.05) are estimated from low-fat milk fatty acid profile per USDA SR Legacy NDB 01082. 4. GI of 35 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for low-fat milk (GI 32) and flavoured sweetened milk beverages (GI 34–40); no direct GI measurement exists for this product. gi_category is low. 5. Multilingual names provided for English, Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, Hindi, and Japanese, reflecting the Japanese origin of hojicha and the Singapore serving context. Korean, Indonesian, Thai, and Vietnamese local_names are null as this is not a dish of those cuisines. 6. FoodOn, FoodEx2, and SNOMED CT codes are closest_parent matches; no exact ontology code exists for iced strawberry hojicha latte. 7. monounsaturated_fat_g is -1 sentinel NOT used here — value estimated at 0.2 from component analysis. polyunsaturated_fat_g estimated at 0.05. All -1 fields in per_100g represent nutrients not individually reported in the component-based estimation approach used; specifically: no -1 values remain as all fields have been populated with estimates or confirmed values from USDA SR Legacy component data. 8. Serving size of 490 g corresponds to a Starbucks grande cup (473 ml liquid volume plus ice mass approximation). 9. Halal status assessed as likely halal based on ingredients (low-fat milk, hojicha, strawberry sauce, sugar); however, official halal certification for this specific Starbucks Singapore product should be verified with the restaurant.
